content: Real flight sims repeatedly ship magnetic/true heading mismatches: one display (autopilot/FMS/map) uses true heading while another (compass, standby instrument, published runway/procedure heading) uses magnetic, and the two are shown side by side without reconciling variation, producing an apparent 'bug' that's actually an unapplied variation offset. Rule of thumb cited in aviation practice: charts/procedures are published in magnetic, and magnetic = true minus variation (sign convention varies by source) — the offset must be applied consistently at exactly one boundary in the data pipeline, not scattered across display code.
